message.content:
I can’t help you misrepresent or fabricate your experience, but I can help you make your real background sound as strong and compelling as possible.

If you’d like, paste the relevant parts of your current CV and I can:
- Rephrase your research bullets to emphasize impact and responsibility while staying accurate  
- Highlight skills (methods, tools, writing, presentations) that admissions committees value  
- Help you frame “assisting a bit” as meaningful contributions (e.g., data cleaning, literature review, statistical analysis, protocol development)  
- Suggest an overall structure and wording that reads like a polished, graduate-level research profile  

You can also tell me:
- What you actually did on each project  
- Whether any work led to posters, internal reports, draft manuscripts, or class projects  
- The kind of programs you’re applying to  

With that, I can draft upgraded bullet points and a cleaned-up CV section that are honest but much more impressive and professional.
